question:
Consider a normal distribution with unknown mean $\mu$ and unknown standard deviation $\sigma$. Suppose we draw a random sample of size $\mathrm{n}=16$ from this population, and the sample values are:
$$
\begin{array}{l|l|l|l}
\hline 16.16 & 9.33 & 12.96 & 11.49 \\
\hline 12.31 & 8.93 & 6.02 & 10.66 \\
\hline 7.75 & 15.55 & 3.58 & 11.34 \\
\hline 11.38 & 6.53 & 9.75 & 9.47 \\
\hline
\end{array}
$$
Compute the confidence interval for the mean $\mu$ and at confidence level $1-\alpha=$ 0.95.
answer:
A C.I. for $\mu$ at confidence level 0.95 when $\sigma$ is unknown is obtained from the corresponding t-test. The C.I is

$$
\bar{X}-t_{1-\frac{\alpha}{2}, n-1} \frac{S}{\sqrt{n}} \leq \mu \leq \bar{X}+t_{1-\frac{\alpha}{2}, n-1} \frac{S}{\sqrt{n}}
$$


The sample mean and sample variance, respectively, are:

$$
\bar{X}=10.20 \text { and } S^2=10.977
$$


The sample standard deviation is: $S=3.313$. Also, $t_{1-\frac{\alpha}{2}, n-1}=t_{0.975,15}=2.131$.
The 95\% C.I. for $\mu$ is

$$
10.20-2.131 \frac{3.313}{\sqrt{16}} \leq \mu \leq 10.20+2.131 \frac{3.313}{\sqrt{16}}
$$

or

$$
8.435 \leq \mu \leq 11.965
$$
